core_mechanism: Exact reduction of an interacting quadratic system to non-interacting (free) modes coupled to a static discrete (Z_2) gauge field, whose gapped phases are then classified by an integer topological invariant (a Chern number) that governs the exchange/braiding statistics of emergent excitations and boundary transport
brief_reason: The abstract states the interacting spin model is solved exactly by reduction to free fermions in a static Z_2 gauge field, with its gapped phases characterized by a spectral Chern number whose value mod 16 fixes the (Abelian/non-Abelian) braiding of excitations and governs edge thermal transport.
